findings.description: strptime with %Y-%m-%d accepts non-zero-padded and variable-width components (e.g. '1-2-3'), contradicting the docstring's strict 'YYYY-MM-DD' claim.
If a datetime (a date subclass) is passed, start_of_day silently discards its time-of-day component instead of rejecting or preserving it.
business_days_between materializes the full day_range list rather than counting weekdays arithmetically, giving O(n) memory for large spans.
day_range builds the entire date list eagerly, which can be memory-heavy for very large ranges where a generator would suffice.
clamp_to_range does not validate timezone-awareness consistency of value/lower/upper (unlike humanize_delta), so a naive/aware mix raises an opaque TypeError.
humanize_delta caps granularity at weeks, so very old moments render as large unbounded week counts (e.g. '520 w ago') rather than months/years.
is_weekend lacks a docstring while every other public function in the module has one, an inconsistency in documentation coverage.
